what_it_does: Proposes targeted augmentations to dna/sequence.json (and routes within-page attention rules to dna/motion.json) based on a 160-page source slice, including new rules, extensions, computed constraints, conflict flags, and open questions for a human decision-maker.
observations: It is an interpretive ingest face, not a code file; symbols[] is empty.
It contains 11 augmentations, each with target, name, value_or_recipe, rationale, provenance, status, and conflict_note.
Statuses include extends-existing, new, and conflicts-with-existing.
Within-page attention mechanics are deliberately routed to motion.json instead of being piled into sequence.json.
It adds a new_sections entry for audience-keyed arc variants.
It lists computed_rules as formal constraints rather than fixed values.
Two items are explicitly not merged: a stat-scale escalation conflict and an opacity-encodes-temporality field that is named but corpus-unevidenced.
It ends with five open questions directed to Tim.
Provenance is cited by collection names and page references, e.g. recent-pitches, deck1-2026, capital-raise, product-vt.
It cross-references other DNA faces: canon.json, voice.json, connectors.json, reader.json, and motion.json.
standouts: Connective-clause title recipe: author the title chain as one sentence, then split at clause boundaries.
Data-slide funnel with a monotone-increasing headline-figure constraint.
Compression-invariance: compress a deck by omitting whole slides, never by thinning a slide's contents.
Audience-keyed arc variants splitting professional/comparison grammar from journey/lifestyle grammar.
Explicit conflict flag on stat-scale escalation instead of silently merging.
conventions: Follows a structured augmentation schema with target, name, value_or_recipe, rationale, provenance, status, and conflict_note.
Uses neutral, operational language and avoids ranking words.
Cites provenance collections and page references for each proposed rule.
Routes rules to the appropriate face and flags conflicts rather than merging them.
Separates computed rules, new sections, and open questions into distinct blocks.
concerns: Some augmentations blend generative recipes with lint/checker extensions, which may blur resolver boundaries.
opacity-encodes-temporality is named in the brief but not evidenced in the 160-page slice, leaving it unresolved.
The stat-scale conflict and compression-invariance vs FUSE tension require human reconciliation.
Several augmentations depend on state in cross-file DNA faces that is not present in this file.
notes: A design-DNA ingest proposal that adds sequence-scale grammar, routes attention rules to motion.json, and explicitly flags conflicts for human resolution.
questions: What is the exact schema of the target files (dna/sequence.json, dna/motion.json) that these augmentations nest into?
Are statuses like extends-existing and conflicts-with-existing part of a formal workflow or ad-hoc labels?
How are provenance page references encoded and verified in the ledger?
What is the decision mechanism for the open questions flagged for Tim?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to add an interpretive layer for a ledger of code/design substrates; the file does exactly that by summarizing source-slice findings and proposing structured augmentations, so it matches.
intention_signals: It tries to turn a corpus review into precise, actionable design-DNA patches while refusing to silently overwrite existing rules, keeping the ledger honest about conflicts and scope boundaries.
novelty: The explicit computed-constraint style for sequence-scale rules (monotone figures, density decline) and the compression-invariance atom-of-compression principle are relatively unusual compared to typical style-guide documents.
contribution: A structured set of proposed sequence-scale grammar rules, computed constraints, audience-arc variants, conflict flags, and open questions derived from a 160-page source slice, with clear provenance and routing to the appropriate DNA faces.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a proposed interpretive ingest face for counterpart-design that maps a 160-page source slice into targeted augmentations for dna/sequence.json and dna/motion.json, including new computed constraints, extensions, audience-keyed arc variants, and explicit conflict flags with open questions for a human decision-maker.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to translate corpus observations into precise, face-routed design-DNA rule updates while preserving conflicts and scope boundaries for later reconciliation.
